evals.prompt: Add ID Capture to my Capacitor app so I can scan passports, driver's licenses and national ID cards and read the holder's name and date of birth. Here's my entry file: EmptyApp.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill writes a complete ID Capture integration: ScanditCaptureCorePlugin.initializePlugins() at startup, DataCaptureContext.initialize(licenseKey), IdCaptureSettings with acceptedDocuments and a FullDocumentScanner wrapped in IdCaptureScanner, new IdCapture(settings) + object-literal listener (didCaptureId/didRejectId), Camera.withSettings(IdCapture.createRecommendedCameraSettings()), DataCaptureView.forContext + view.connectToElement on a #data-capture-view div, and a Capacitor App-plugin lifecycle.

assertions.text: '-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --' is present
settings.acceptedDocuments is configured with document objects like Passport, DriverLicense, IdCard (semantic)
the listener is a plain object literal with didCaptureId and didRejectId methods, not a class implementing an interface (semantic)
addListener is called on the IdCapture instance, awaited (semantic)
the IdCapture mode is attached to the context via context.setMode or context.addMode, with the call awaited (semantic)
connectToElement( is called on the view (semantic)
Setup checklist mentions NSCameraUsageDescription for iOS (semantic)
Setup checklist mentions the @capacitor/camera plugin or the runtime camera permission flow (semantic)
settings.scannerType is NOT used in the final code (semantic)
IdCaptureRegion.Any or another PascalCase IdCaptureRegion member is used, not the lowercase Flutter-style form (semantic)

evals.prompt: I only need to read the PDF417 barcode on the back of US driver's licenses — nothing else. Set up ID Capture for that in EmptyApp.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill accepts only DriverLicense for IdCaptureRegion.Us and uses a SingleSideScanner with only the barcode flag enabled, wrapped in IdCaptureScanner.

assertions.text: the SingleSideScanner enables only the barcode flag, i.e. SingleSideScanner(true, false, false) (semantic)
data from the barcode is read, either via capturedId.barcode or via the harmonized top-level fields (documentNumber, fullName, dateOfBirth, etc.) that the barcode populates (semantic)

evals.prompt: Set up ID Capture to scan passports and extract the MRZ data (full name, document number, date of expiry). Use EmptyApp.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill accepts Passport, uses a scanner that reads the MRZ, and reads fields from the captured id / mrz result. The MRZ result is accessed via capturedId.mrzResult (not capturedId.mrz, which is the Flutter name).

assertions.text: the scanner reads the MRZ, via FullDocumentScanner or a SingleSideScanner with machineReadableZone enabled (semantic)
capturedId.mrz is NOT used (the Flutter name) (semantic)

evals.prompt: Add ID Capture but anonymize sensitive data — I don't want document images or sensitive fields in the result. Use EmptyApp.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets IdCaptureSettings.anonymizationMode to an IdAnonymizationMode value that covers fields and images.

assertions.text: an anonymization mode covering fields and images is chosen, e.g. IdAnonymizationMode.FieldsAndImages (semantic)

evals.prompt: I need the cropped document image and the face image back from each scanned ID, and I want to render them in an tag. Set that up in EmptyApp.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables image extraction via setShouldPassImageTypeToResult for IdImageType.Face and IdImageType.CroppedDocument, reads capturedId.images, and renders the base64 strings via the data: URI form (since IdImages returns base64 strings on Capacitor, not URIs or HTMLImageElements).

assertions.text: the cropped document image is read via images.getCroppedDocument(IdSide.Front) — not a non-existent .croppedDocument getter (semantic)
the base64 image is rendered using the data:image/png;base64, URI form on an element, not via .buffer / .bytes (semantic)

evals.prompt: We want to reject voided / cancelled US driver's licenses during ID Capture. How do I enable that? My entry file is EmptyApp.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds the scandit-capacitor-datacapture-id-voided-detection package, sets settings.rejectVoidedIds = true, and handles RejectionReason.DocumentVoided in didRejectId.

assertions.text: the scandit-capacitor-datacapture-id-voided-detection package is added as a dependency (semantic)

evals.prompt: We scan European driving licenses and need the vehicle categories from the back of the card. Set up ID Capture for that in EmptyApp.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds the scandit-capacitor-datacapture-id-europe-driving-license package, sets settings.decodeBackOfEuropeanDrivingLicense = true with a scanner that reads the back, and reads capturedId.vizResult.drivingLicenseDetails.drivingLicenseCategories, using category.code (the documented getter), not the non-existent categoryCode.

assertions.text: each category's data is read via the .code getter, not a non-existent .categoryCode (semantic)
a scanner that reads the back of the card is used, e.g. FullDocumentScanner (semantic)
the scandit-capacitor-datacapture-id-europe-driving-license package is added as a dependency (semantic)

evals.prompt: Add forged-barcode detection for US driver's licenses (AAMVA) to my ID Capture setup so we can flag likely-forged IDs. Use EmptyApp.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds the scandit-capacitor-datacapture-id-aamva-barcode-verification package, sets settings.rejectForgedAamvaBarcodes = true, and/or reads capturedId.verificationResult.aamvaBarcodeVerification with AamvaBarcodeVerificationStatus. It does NOT use a standalone AamvaBarcodeVerifier class (removed on Capacitor at v8).

assertions.text: verificationResult.aamvaBarcodeVerification is referenced, or RejectionReason.ForgedAamvaBarcode is handled (semantic)
AamvaBarcodeVerificationStatus is referenced when inspecting the result (semantic)
the scandit-capacitor-datacapture-id-aamva-barcode-verification package is added as a dependency (semantic)
a standalone AamvaBarcodeVerifier class is NOT used in the code (semantic)

evals.prompt: How should I handle the case where a document is detected but can't be captured? I want to show the user a clear message. My entry file is EmptyApp.ts and I accept passports and driver's licenses.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements didRejectId and maps RejectionReason values (Timeout, NotAcceptedDocumentType, etc.) to user-facing messages, disabling capture while the message is shown.

assertions.text: rejection reasons are mapped to user-facing messages, e.g. via a switch on RejectionReason (semantic)
idCapture.isEnabled is set to false while the rejection message is shown (semantic)

evals.prompt: I scan US driver's licenses and want to make sure the printed (VIZ) data matches the data encoded in the PDF417 barcode — reject the document if they don't agree, and on a successful scan I want to inspect which consistency checks passed. Use EmptyApp.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.rejectInconsistentData = true (so a mismatch surfaces as RejectionReason.InconsistentData in didRejectId), and in didCaptureId reads capturedId.verificationResult.dataConsistency, inspecting dataConsistency.allChecksPassed.

evals.prompt: I need to scan mobile driver's licenses (mDL, ISO 18013-5) in my Capacitor app and read the holder's name and date of birth. Set that up in EmptyApp.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures settings.scanner = new IdCaptureScanner(undefined, new MobileDocumentScanner(true, false)) for mobile documents, and reads the mobile-document data via capturedId.mobileDocument (or the harmonized top-level fields). MobileDocumentScanner ships in the base package, with no add-on dependency.

assertions.text: the MobileDocumentScanner is passed as the second positional argument to IdCaptureScanner, i.e. IdCaptureScanner(undefined, new MobileDocumentScanner(...)) (semantic)
capturedId.mobileDocument is read, or the harmonized top-level fields the mobile document populates are read (semantic)
no separate add-on package is added for mobile documents — MobileDocumentScanner is in the base package (semantic)
no AamvaBarcodeVerifier and no IdDocumentType bitmask are used for mobile documents (semantic)

evals.prompt: I'm building an airport check-in screen in my Capacitor app. On one screen I need to scan the PDF417 barcode on a boarding pass AND scan the passenger's passport — both with the same camera preview. Set that up in EmptyApp.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill keeps one DataCaptureContext, one DataCaptureView and one camera, and runs IdCapture and BarcodeCapture together. It attaches BOTH modes with context.addMode(idCapture) and context.addMode(barcodeCapture) — NOT context.setMode (which replaces the current mode). Each mode gets its own listener (IdCapture's didCaptureId/didRejectId; BarcodeCapture's didScan reading session.newlyRecognizedBarcode), BarcodeCapture enables Symbology.PDF417, and both modes are toggled via isEnabled. Co-existence is presented as supported.

assertions.text: context.addMode(idCapture) is called to attach the ID Capture mode
context.addMode(barcodeCapture) is called to attach the Barcode Capture mode to the SAME context
context.setMode( is NOT used to attach these two modes — setMode replaces the current mode rather than adding, so it cannot run both together (semantic)
new IdCapture( and new BarcodeCapture( are both constructed
the IdCapture mode has its own listener with didCaptureId
the BarcodeCapture mode has its own listener with didScan that reads session.newlyRecognizedBarcode
BarcodeCaptureSettings enables Symbology.PDF417 (the IATA boarding-pass symbology)
running IdCapture and BarcodeCapture together on one DataCaptureContext is presented as supported, not as something that must be avoided (semantic)
